revision.comment: 新しいページ：曽根田白塚古墳（広島県福山市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 曽根田白塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = 曾根田白塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[芦田町]]大字下有地 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 24.35 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 16|経度秒 = 2.00 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径9m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「曽根田白塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 曽根田白塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''曽根田白塚古墳'''（そねだしらつかこふん、曾根田白塚古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[芦田町]]下有地（しもあるじ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]南岸において久田谷の北側丘陵の頂上付近に築造された古墳である。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径約9メートルを測る[http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html 曽根田白塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南方向に開口する。[[花崗岩]]の切石を使用した整美な石槨であり、隙間には漆喰が残存する。副葬品は詳らかでない。 この曽根田白塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[尾市1号墳]]（福山市新市町常）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との強い結びつきを示唆するとして重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石槨の規模は次の通り。 * 石槨部：長さ2.19-2.20メートル、幅1.22-1.23メートル、現在高さ1.22メートル（奥） * 羨道部：長さ3.64-3.75メートル、幅1.80-1.81メートル、現在高さ1.1メートル（開口部） 石槨の石材には[[花崗岩]]の切石が使用される。石槨部の東側石には意図的に亀裂が入れられており、西側石と対称的になることが意識される。石の隙間には漆喰が残存するほか、石槨部には底石が無い点で特異な石槨になる。 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 曽根田白塚古墳 - 1981年（昭和56年）4月17日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=白塚古墳 > 曽根田白塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|曽根田白塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[尾市1号墳]] * [[猪ノ子古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:そねたしらつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 曽根田白塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・[[横口式石槨]]開口部 |別名 = 曾根田白塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[芦田町]]大字下有地 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 24.35 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 16|経度秒 = 2.00 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径9m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「曽根田白塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 曽根田白塚

1981年（昭和56年）4月17日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=白塚古墳 > 曽根田白塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|曽根田白塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[白石太一郎]]|year=2007|chapter=備後の横口式石槨をめぐって|title=大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館館報 11|publisher=[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白石太一郎|2007}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[尾市1号墳]] * [[猪ノ子古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:そねたしらつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''曽根田白塚古墳'''（そねだしらつかこふん、曾根田白塚古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[芦田町]]下有地（しもあるじ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]南岸において久田谷の北側丘陵の頂上付近に築造された小円墳である。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径約9メートルを測る[http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html 曽根田白塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南方向に開口する。[[花崗岩]]の切石を使用した整美な石槨であり、隙間には漆喰が残存する。副葬品は詳らかでない。 この曽根田白塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[尾市1号墳]]（福山市新市町常）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との強い結びつきを示唆するとして重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[横口式石槨]] 羨道部・石槨部}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石槨の規模は次の通り。 * 石槨部：長さ2.19-2.20メートル、幅1.22-1.23メートル、現在高さ1.22メートル（奥） * 羨道部：長さ3.64-3.75メートル、幅1.80-1.81メートル、現在高さ1.1メートル（開口部） 石槨の石材には[[花崗岩]]の切石が使用される。石槨部の東側石には意図的に亀裂が入れられており、西側石と対称的になることが意識される。石の隙間には漆喰が残存するほか、石槨部には底石が無い点で特異な石槨になる（横穴式石室から横口式石槨への変容を示す{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}）。 File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|石槨部（奥壁方向） File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道部（開口部方向） File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 曽根田白塚古墳 - 1981年（昭和56年）4月17日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=白塚古墳 > 曽根田白塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|曽根田白塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=白石太一郎|authorlink=白石太一郎|year=2007|chapter=備後の横口式石槨をめぐって|title=大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館館報 11|publisher=[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白石太一郎|2007}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[尾市1号墳]] * [[猪ノ子古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:そねたしらつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 曽根田白塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・[[横口式石槨]]開口部 |別名 = 曾根田白塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[芦田町]]大字下有地 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 24.35 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 16|経度秒 = 2.00 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径9m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「曽根田白塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 曽根田白塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''曽根田白塚古墳'''（そねだしらつかこふん、曾根田白塚古墳）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[芦田町]]下有地（しもあるじ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[芦田川]]南岸において久田谷の北側丘陵の頂上付近に築造された小円墳である。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径約9メートルを測る[http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html 曽根田白塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南方向に開口する。[[花崗岩]]の切石を使用した整美な石槨であり、隙間には漆喰が遺存する。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される。横口式石槨古墳の多くは畿内に限られるが、備後地方は本古墳のほか[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）・[[尾市1号墳]]（福山市新市町常）でも横口式石槨を採用する点で特異性を示しており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との強い結びつきを示唆するとして重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石槨俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石槨の規模は次の通り。 * 石槨部：長さ2.19-2.20メートル、幅1.22-1.23メートル、現在高さ1.22メートル（奥） * 羨道部：長さ3.64-3.75メートル、幅1.80-1.81メートル、現在高さ1.1メートル（開口部） 石槨の石材には[[花崗岩]]の切石が使用される。石槨部の東側石には意図的に亀裂が入れられており、西側石と対称的になることが意識される。石の隙間には漆喰が残存するほか、石槨部には底石が無い点で特異な石槨になる（横穴式石室から横口式石槨への変容を示す{{Sfn|白石太一郎|2007}}）。 Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道部・石槨部 Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|石槨部（奥壁方向） Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道部（開口部方向） Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 曽根田白塚古墳 - 1981年（昭和56年）4月17日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=白塚古墳 > 曽根田白塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=白石太一郎|authorlink=白石太一郎|year=2007|chapter=備後の横口式石槨をめぐって|title=大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館館報|issue=11|publisher=[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白石太一郎|2007}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[尾市1号墳]] * [[猪ノ子古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html 曽根田白塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:そねたしらつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]]

Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New pages]] [[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:New pages]][[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]]

Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]]

Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka revision.text:
{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima
