{{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}} * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | shape = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round burial mound|円墳]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular burial mound is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}.
