revision.comment: Text replacement - "|円墳" to "|円墳|1=Round Kofun"

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun}}* Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | construction_period = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its shape is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References revision.comment: Text replacement - "|横穴式石室" to "|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | artifacts = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 32 | latitude_sec = 24.35 | longitude_deg = 133 | longitude_min = 16 | longitude_sec = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | icon = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its dimensions are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates_deg = 34 | coordinates_min = 32 | coordinates_sec = 24.35 | coordinates_deg = 133 | coordinates = 16 | coordinates = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates_deg = 34 | coordinates_min = 32 | coordinates_sec = 24.35 | coordinates_deg = 133 | coordinates = 16 | coordinates = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | label_position = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 24.35 | coordinates = 133 | coordinates = 16 | coordinates = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural other_other_designation "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 24.35 | coordinates = 133 | coordinates = 16 | coordinates = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural other_other_designation "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == Burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | other_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 24.35 | coordinates = 133 | coordinates = 16 | coordinates = 2.00 | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = [[Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|ja|芦田川}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the [[7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}}, and {{ill|Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was other_designation1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural other_designation in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial Structure == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}.
