[[co:commons:Category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q104660562]] Wikidata name: Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | alternate_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.54011111111111|N|133.26722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the [[:en:Ashida River|Ashida River]] in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut [[:en:Granite|granite]] stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]], emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut [[:en:Granite|granite]] blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | alternate_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.54011111111111|N|133.26722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the [[:en:Ashida River|Ashida River]] in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut [[:en:Granite|granite]] stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]], emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut [[:en:Granite|granite]] blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|version=104540319|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q104660562}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, around the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | alternate_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.54011111111111|N|133.26722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|en|Ashida River|WD=Q2577132|lt=Ashida River}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, around the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|en|Ashida River|WD=Q2577132|lt=Ashida River}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court}}, emphasizing its historical significance. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun]][[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | alternate_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.54011111111111|N|133.26722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|en|Ashida River|WD=Q2577132|lt=Ashida River}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, around the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|version=104540319|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:曽根田白塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q104660562}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | alternate_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.54011111111111|N|133.26722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|en|Ashida River|WD=Q2577132|lt=Ashida River|1=Ashida River}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway and chamber Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Interior of the chamber (toward the back wall) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the opening) Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Opening to the chamber == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical site explanation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|authorlink=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report|issue=11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}}* {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120910.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] - Mound and opening to the [[horizontal stone chamber]] | alternate_name = 曾根田白塚古墳 | affiliation = | location = Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.54011111111111|N|133.26722222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso_code = JP-34 | type = [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] | size = Diameter 9m | burial_structure = {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = [[Late 7th century]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Soneda Shiratsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun''' (曽根田白塚古墳, そねだしらつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Shimoruji, Ashida Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This small circular Kofun is situated near the summit of a hill on the northern side of the Hisada Valley, south of the {{ill|Ashida River|en|Ashida River|WD=Q2577132|lt=Ashida River|1=Ashida River}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. It has not undergone archaeological excavation. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 9 meters[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64158.html Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage).. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber, made of precisely cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}} stones, features residual plaster in the gaps between stones. Details about the grave goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. While horizontal stone chambers are rare outside Kinai, the Bitchu region, including this kofun, the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, showcases a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}}, emphasizing its historical significance. The mound area was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in {{date|1981}} ({{era|Showa|56}}). == burial burial == [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Chamber: Length 2.19–2.20 meters, width 1.22–1.23 meters, current height 1.22 meters (at the back) * Passageway: Length 3.64–3.75 meters, width 1.80–1.81 meters, current height 1.1 meters (at the opening) The stone chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}} blocks. Intentional cracks on the east side of the chamber stones are mirrored symmetrically on the west side. Residual plaster is found in the gaps between the stones. The chamber notably lacks a bottom stone, making it an unusual example of burial architecture, likely demonstrating a transition from {{ill|corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} to horizontal stone chambers{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}.
